item.title: Microsoft Dynamics 365
item.description: Microsoft Dynamics 365 is a comprehensive cloud-based business application suite integrating ERP, CRM, and AI-driven insights. It streamlines operations, enhances customer engagement, and provides data-driven decision-making for businesses of all sizes, fostering growth and efficiency.
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft Dynamics 365 is a comprehensive cloud-based business application suite integrating ERP, CRM, and AI-driven insights. It streamlines operations, enhances customer engagement, and provides data-driven decision-making for businesses of all sizes, fostering growth and efficiency.

Overview

In an earlier blog, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Collections we looked into collecting accounts and entitlements from Microsoft Dynamics 365. In this article, we will discuss provisioning into Microsoft Dynamics 365 using REST APIs.

Connector Settings

Steps

  1. Login to RSA Identity G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to AFX > Connectors.
  3. Click on Create Connector and select the RESTful Web Service connector template.
  4. Enter the details as shown below under General tab.

  5. Click on the Settings tab and enter the required connection details.

  6. Click on Add More Parameters.
  7. Enter the details for scope as shown below and click OK.

  8. Provide the https://instance.dynamics.com/.default offline_access in the Scope field.
  9. Click on Get OAuth 2.0 Access Token and authorize.
  10. Click OK.

Supported Verbs

Login

💡Note

If your setup requires the client credentials flow of OAuth, it is advised to use the Login verb.

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Login and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
ClientIDString✔️Client ID
ClientSecretString✔️✔️Client Secret
Command Output Parameters
Defaults
Command Code
MethodPOST
Pathhttps://login.microsoftonline.com/TENANT_ID/oauth2/token
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
Request Body
grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=${ClientID}&client_secret=${ClientSecret}&Resource=https://instance.dynamics.com/
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Session Token
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionaccess_token
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Create an Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Create an Account and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
EmailString✔️Email${AccountTemplate.Email}
UserIDString✔️User ID${AccountTemplate.Account_Name}
UserNameString✔️User Name${AccountTemplate.UserName}
AliasStringAlias${AccountTemplate.Alias}
AccountTypeStringAccount Type${AccountTemplate.AccountType}
NetworkDomainStringNetwork Domain${AccountTemplate.NetworkDomain}
Command Output Parameters
Parameter NameTypeDisplay NameMappingDescription
AccountNameStringAccount NameAccount.NameAccount name for the new user
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPOST
Path/data/SystemUsers
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "UserID": "${UserID}",
  "UserName": "${UserName}",
  "Email": "${Email}",
  "UserInfo_language": "en-us",
  "Helplanguage": "en-us",
  "Alias": "${Alias}",
  "AccountType": "${AccountType}",
  "NetworkDomain": "${NetworkDomain}",
  "Enabled": "true"
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression(?s)"detail"\s*:\s*"([^"]*)"
Pattern
(.*)Here's the details: $1
AccountName
Expression TypeStatus Code
Expression
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Delete an Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Delete an Account and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
AccountString✔️Account${Account.Name}
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodDELETE
Path/data/SystemUsers(UserID='${UserID}')
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/json
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
-
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Pattern
(.*)Details: $1
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Enable an Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Enable an Account and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
AccountString✔️Account${Account.Name}
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPATCH
Path/data/SystemUsers(UserID='${UserID}')
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/json
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "Enabled": true
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Pattern
(.*)Details: $1
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Disable an Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Disable an Account and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
AccountString✔️Account${Account.Name}
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPATCH
Path/data/SystemUsers(UserID='${UserID}')
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/json
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "Enabled": false
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Pattern
(.*)Details: $1
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Add Application Role to Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Add Application Role to Account and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
AccountString✔️Account${Account.Name}
AccountString✔️Security Role Identifier${ApplicationRole.Name}
AccountStringSecurity Role Name${ApplicationRole.Role_Name}
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPOST
Path/data/SecurityUserRoles
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/json
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "UserId": "${Account}",
  "SecurityRoleIdentifier": "${SecurityRoleIdentifier}",
  "SecurityRoleName": "${SecurityRoleName}"
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess (Partial Match ✔️)
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Pattern
(.*)Details: $1
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Remove Application Role from Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Remove Application Role from Account and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
AccountString✔️Account${Account.Name}
AccountString✔️Security Role Identifier${ApplicationRole.Name}
AccountStringSecurity Role Name${ApplicationRole.Role_Name}
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodDELETE
Path/data/SecurityUserRoles(UserId='${UserId}',SecurityRoleIdentifier='${SecurityRoleIdentifier}')
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/json
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "UserId": "${Account}",
  "SecurityRoleIdentifier": "${SecurityRoleIdentifier}",
  "SecurityRoleName": "${SecurityRoleName}"
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess (Partial Match ✔️)
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Pattern
(.*)Details: $1
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Add Application Role to a Group

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Add Application Role to a Group and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
GroupString✔️Group${Group.Name}
SecurityRoleIdentifierString✔️Security Role Identifier${ApplicationRole.Name}
SecurityRoleNameStringSecurity Role Name${ApplicationRole.Role_Name}
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPOST
Path/data/SecurityUserRoles
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/json
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "UserId": "${Group}",
  "SecurityRoleIdentifier": "${SecurityRoleIdentifier}",
  "SecurityRoleName": "${SecurityRoleName}"
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess (Partial Match ✔️)
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Pattern
(.*)Details: $1
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Remove Application Role from a Group

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Remove Application Role from a Group and configure as shown below.
Command Input Parameters
Parameter NameTypeRequiredEncryptedDisplay NameMapping
GroupString✔️Group${Group.Name}
SecurityRoleIdentifierString✔️Security Role Identifier${ApplicationRole.Name}
SecurityRoleNameStringSecurity Role Name${ApplicationRole.Role_Name}
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodDELETE
Path/data/SecurityUserRoles(UserId='${UserId}',SecurityRoleIdentifier='${SecurityRoleIdentifier}')
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/json
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "UserId": "${Group}",
  "SecurityRoleIdentifier": "${SecurityRoleIdentifier}",
  "SecurityRoleName": "${SecurityRoleName}"
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
SUCCESSShouldn't match
SUCCESSSuccess (Partial Match ✔️)
(.*)Failed ($1)
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Pattern
(.*)Details: $1
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

item.title: MangoApps
item.description: MangoApps is a versatile collaboration platform that enables organizations
item.content:encoded:
MangoApps is a versatile collaboration platform that enables organizations to streamline communication and enhance productivity. It offers a comprehensive suite of features, including instant messaging, file sharing, task management, and team collaboration tools. With its intuitive interface and robust functionality, MangoApps empowers teams to connect, share ideas, and work together seamlessly across departments and geographies. Whether it's project coordination, knowledge sharing, or social engagement, MangoApps provides a unified platform that fosters efficient and effective collaboration within businesses of all sizes.

MangoApps Homepage

Overview

In an earlier blog, MangoApps Collections we looked into collecting accounts and entitlements from MangoApps. In this article, we will discuss provisioning into MangoApps using REST APIs.

Connector Settings

Steps

  1. Login to RSA Identity G&L console as System Administrator.
  2. Navigate to AFX > Connectors.
  3. Click on Create Connector and select the RESTful Web Service connector template.
  4. Enter the details as shown below under General tab.

  5. Click on the Settings tab and enter the required connection details.

  6. Click OK. We will configure individual verbs for this connector in the subsequent sections.

Supported Verbs

Login

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Login and configure as shown below.

Command Input Parameters

Parameter NameDisplay NameDescription
Username✔️UsernameService account user name
Password✔️ 🔒PasswordPassword for the service account
ClientID✔️Client IDClient ID for the API access
ClientSecret✔️ 🔒Client SecretSecret corresponding to the Client ID

🔒 = Encrypted ✔️ = Required

Command Output Parameters
Defaults
Command Code
MethodPOST
Pathoauth2/token.json
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
Request Body

client_id=${ClientID}&client_secret=${ClientSecret} &grant_type=password&username=${Username}&password=${Password}

Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionerror
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionerror_description
Session Token
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionaccess_token
  1. Click OK. 4. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Create an Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Create an Account and configure as shown below.

Command Input Parameters

Parameter NameDisplay NameMappingDescription
Email✔️Email Address${AccountTemplate.Email}Primary email address of user
FName✔️First Name${AccountTemplate.FirstName}A given name of a person
LName✔️Last Name${AccountTemplate.LastName}Surname of a person
EmployeeID✔️Employee ID${AccountTemplate.EmployeeID}User's employee ID in the organization
TitleTitle${AccountTemplate.Title}Job title for the user
CostCenterCost Center${AccountTemplate.CostCenter}User's cost center

🔒 = Encrypted ✔️ = Required

Command Output Parameters
Defaults
Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPOST
Pathapi/v2/websync/users.json
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
   "email" : "${Email}" 
   "firstName" : "${FName}" 
   "lastName" : "${LName}" 
   "usertype" : "Network User" 
   "title" : "${Title}" 
   "employeeid" : "${EmployeeID}" 
   "costcenter" : "${CostCenter}" 
   "teammemberstatus" : "Member" 
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionmessage
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Status Code
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Content
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Message
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
Detail
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Add Account to Group

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Add Account to Group and configure as shown below.

    info

    The MangoApps APIs to add account to group does not support passing account name (email) and hence we cannot use this in conjunction with Create Account. Create account does not return account ID and hence will have to wait for data collection.

Command Input Parameters

Parameter NameDisplay NameMappingDescription
AccountId✔️Account ID${Account.External_Id}Unique identifier for the user
GroupId✔️Group ID${Group.External_Id}Unique identifier for the group

🔒 = Encrypted ✔️ = Required

Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPUT
Pathapi/groups/${GroupId}/members/manage.json?send_member_list=false
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "ms_request": {
    "group": {
      "add_member_ids": [
        "${AccountId}"
      ],
      "generate_feed": "Y"
    }
  }
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionmessage
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Remove Account from Group

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Remove Account from Group and configure as shown below.

Command Input Parameters

Parameter NameDisplay NameMappingDescription
AccountId✔️Account ID${Account.External_Id}Unique identifier for the user
GroupId✔️Group ID${Group.External_Id}Unique identifier for the group

🔒 = Encrypted ✔️ = Required

Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPUT
Pathapi/groups/${GroupId}/members/manage.json?send_member_list=false
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "ms_request": {
    "group": {
      "remove_member_ids": [
        "${AccountId}"
      ],
      "generate_feed": "Y"
    }
  }
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionmessage
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.

Update an Account

Steps

  1. Edit the connector and navigate to Capabilities tab.
  2. Click on Update an Account and configure as shown below.

Command Input Parameters

Parameter NameDisplay NameMappingDescription
Email✔️Email Address${User.Email}Primary email address of user
FNameFirst Name${User.FirstName}A given name of a person
LNameLast Name${User.LastName}Surname of a person
EmployeeIDEmployee ID${User.EmployeeID}User's employee ID in the organization
TitleTitle${User.Title}Job title for the user
CostCenterCost Center${User.CostCenter}User's cost center

🔒 = Encrypted ✔️ = Required

Command Code
Generate SessionToken✔️
MethodPOST
Pathapi/v2/websync/users.json
Request Headers
Content-Typeapplication/x-www-form-urlencoded
Acceptapplication/json; charset=utf-8
AuthorizationBearer ${SessionToken}
Request Body
{
  "email": "${Email}",
  "firstName": "${FName}",
  "lastName": "${FName}",
  "usertype": "Network User",
  "title": "${Title}",
  "employeeid": "${EmployeeID}",
  "costcenter": "${CostCenter}",
  "teammemberstatus": "Member"
}
Status Code
Expression TypeStatus Code
Pattern
^[23]\d2$0
^([45])\d2$$1
Brief Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expressionmessage
Detailed Response
Expression TypeJson Path
Expression
  1. Click OK.
  2. Run the Test Connector Capabilities to verify the verb.
